# Stroom Performance — Full AI Context

> High-altitude drag racing parts store. Differentiated by altitude-aware fitment
> (density altitude / DA, hypoxia, altitude tuning) for No-Prep and Index racing.
> Grew out of the Stroom Performance YouTube channel. Commerce is Shopify-backed
> and drop-shipped; catalog + checkout launch in stages as supplier accounts open.
> Wholesale/dealer inquiries are open now — attracting suppliers is the current goal.

## The niche (why Stroom exists)
Most performance stores implicitly assume sea level. At high density altitude the
air is thinner: the engine fights hypoxia, torque-converter stall shifts, boost is
leaned on to recover power, and fuel delivery math changes. Stroom's moat is
answering "will this part work for MY application at MY altitude?" — a question no
general parts store answers well. Every listing carries Stroom-authored fitment and
DA notes, confidence-tagged (verified-on-car > vendor-stated > inferred). This is
Stroom-owned data, not supplier passthrough.

## Object model (for grounding)
- Product (reference; Shopify-owned once live): name, brand, system, racing styles, price, availability, specs, fitment.
- Category (reference; two facets): Shop by System, Shop by Racing Style.
- Fitment (junction Product↔Application; the critical object): application, DA range, required mods, confidence, notes.
- Guide / How-To (site-owned): steps, tools, parts used, difficulty, video.
- Build (site-owned; scope being finalized): the shop car documenting parts at altitude.
- Supplier/Brand (drop-ship source): Squash Performance, Motion Race Works, Holley, converter/safety partners.
- Order / Customer / Cart (Shopify-owned system objects): checkout handled entirely by Shopify.

## Shop by System
### Fuel — /collections/fuel
Intank hats, primary/secondary pumps, relays, hoses. Thin air needs more fuel volume
for the same power; delivery must be sized for it. Trap-door access mod documented in guides.
### Engine — /collections/engine
Valley plates, gaskets, head gaskets, catch cans. Ventilation and sealing under boost.
### Drivetrain — /collections/drivetrain
Torque converters and driveline. Converter stall is altitude-dependent; spec to your DA.
### Electronics — /collections/electronics
EFI (e.g. Holley Terminator X), boost controllers, two/three-step launch control, sensors,
gauges, data logging — the brain that triggers fuel and shapes the boost curve.
### Safety — /collections/safety
Engine diapers and containment for high-boost failures; often track-required.

## Shop by Racing Style
### No Prep — /collections/no-prep
Un-prepped surface; traction never guaranteed. Control-first parts and tuning. Stroom's home discipline.
### Index — /collections/index
Consistency-first for index/bracket classes (e.g. 7.0). Repeatable runs beat one hero pass.
### Street/Strip — /collections/street-strip
Dual-duty combos that survive daily heat cycles and a full altitude pull.

## Representative products (catalog launching soon; availability honest)
### Squash Performance Intank Hat — /products/squash-intank-hat  [system: fuel · coming-soon]
High-flow in-tank hat for larger pump(s) without external plumbing. Fitment: turbo
street/strip and No-Prep builds; good to ~6,600 ft DA; requires trap-door access panel;
confidence verified-on-car.
### Motion Race Works Valley Plate — /products/motion-valley-plate  [system: engine · coming-soon]
Billet, O-ring sealed, PCV/catch-can provisions for boosted LS engines. Confidence vendor-stated.
### High-DA Torque Converter — /products/high-da-torque-converter  [system: drivetrain · build-to-order]
Furnace-brazed converter built to your combo; stall tuned to your home DA (4,000–7,000 ft).
The clearest altitude example — selection depends on application AND altitude. Confidence verified-on-car.
### Holley Terminator X EFI — /products/holley-terminator-x  [system: electronics · coming-soon]
Standalone EFI: secondary-pump trigger, boost control, launch control, built-in data logging.
MAP-enforced pricing. Confidence vendor-stated.
### Engine Diaper — /products/engine-diaper  [system: safety · coming-soon]
Oil/debris containment for high-boost runs; confirm class rating.

## Guides
### Cutting a Trap Door for Fuel-Pump Access — /guides/trap-door-fuel-pump-access  [advanced]
Fabricate a serviceable access panel with a "Death Wheel" angle grinder so an in-tank pump
can be serviced without dropping the tank. Parts used: Squash intank hat.
### High-Altitude Tuning Basics — Reading DA — /guides/high-altitude-tuning-basics  [intermediate]
Read the day's DA; match fuel to air; manage the boost curve; confirm converter behavior.
Parts used: Holley Terminator X, High-DA torque converter.

## Builds
### The Stroom Shop Car — /builds/stroom-shop-car
Home DA ~6,600 ft; No-Prep/Index. The proving ground — every part earned its place here first.

## Wholesale & partnerships (current priority) — /wholesale
Stroom is opening drop-ship and dealer accounts ahead of the public catalog. Value to suppliers:
a built-in high-intent YouTube audience, a niche nobody owns, content that features parts working
at altitude wired to shoppable listings, and a lean drop-ship model. Contact: dealers@stroomperformance.com

## WebMCP tools (navigator.modelContext)
get_products(system?), check_fitment(application, altitudeFt), get_categories(),
find_by_race_style(style), get_guides(), get_wholesale_info().

## Contact
YouTube: Stroom Performance channel. Wholesale: dealers@stroomperformance.com. General: hello@stroomperformance.com.
